name: Are there specific technical SEO requirements for showing up in Google AI Overviews, or is regular optimization enough?

mainEntity.name: Are there specific technical SEO requirements for showing up in Google AI Overviews, or is regular optimization enough?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Google AI Overviews have no unique technical requirements beyond what’s needed for regular search, but several technical best practices significantly improve your chances. Think crawlability, speed, mobile readiness, and structured data.

Because AI Overviews depend on Google understanding and extracting your content reliably, technical issues like slow load times, blocked resources, or messy HTML can lower your odds. Conversely, clean markup and schema make it easier for AI to trust and reuse your page.

Key factors:
- XML sitemaps and proper indexing
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Fast page speed and lightweight code
- Article, FAQPage, HowTo, LocalBusiness schema where relevant

Practically, run a technical SEO audit, fix crawl errors, improve Core Web Vitals, ensure all key content is visible in HTML, and add structured data to your main informational pages. Re-test periodically as Google updates its systems.

Soft positioning: Strong technical SEO won’t guarantee AI Overview placement, but weak technical foundations almost always hold you back.
acceptedAnswer.description: There are no special technical requirements for AI Overviews beyond normal Google SEO, but you’ll perform much better if your site is fast, mobile‑friendly, fully crawlable, and marked up with structured data like Article, FAQPage, and HowTo schema.
